name: Yazid ibn Thubayt
title: Lion of Basra
role: Brave Shia and Father of Martyrs
shortDescription: A prominent Shia elder in Basra who, despite strict road closures, reached Mecca with his two sons to sacrifice themselves for the Imam in Karbala.
fullStory:
Yazid ibn Thubayt al-Abdi was a noble, brave warrior, and prominent Shia elder in the city of Basra. He was considered a loyal companion of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) and held special respect among his people. When Imam Hussain's (AS) letter reached the elders of Basra, Yazid ibn Thubayt was present in the house of a Shia woman (Mariya bint Munqidh), and upon hearing the Imam's message, he made a firm decision to help him.

At that time, Ibn Ziyad (the governor of Basra) tightly controlled all exit routes from the city to prevent anyone from joining Imam Hussain (AS). Yazid ibn Thubayt bravely announced among his ten sons that he intended to join the Imam. Among his children, two named 'Abdullah' and 'Ubaydullah' accompanied him.

Yazid wisely and skillfully bypassed the roadblocks and guards of Basra, traveling through harsh deserts to reach Mecca and join the Imam's caravan. His arrival in Mecca coincided with the Imam preparing to move towards Iraq. Imam Hussain (AS) was very happy to see him and recited the verse 'By the grace of God and His mercy, let them rejoice'.

He and his two sons accompanied the Imam all the way from Mecca to Karbala. On the day of Ashura, Yazid ibn Thubayt witnessed the tragic martyrdom of his two young sons (Abdullah and Ubaydullah) in the mass attack by Umar Sa'd's army. Seeing his children's martyrdom, he not only didn't lose his morale but charged into the battlefield with righteous anger.

Yazid fought with utmost bravery in an epic hand-to-hand combat, inflicting heavy casualties on the enemy until he too attained martyrdom defending Wilayah, becoming eternal alongside his sons. His name and his sons' names are mentioned in the Ziyarat of Nahiya al-Muqaddasa.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى يَزيدَ بْنِ ثُبَيْتٍ العَبْدِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Yazid ibn Thubayt al-Abdi and his two martyred sons.
